title: lycopus rubellus var. arkansanus
intro: Lycopus rubellus var. arkansanus, also known as Arkansas water hedge nettle or American water bugle, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the Lycopus genus. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Lycopus rubellus var. arkansanus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tincture or tea, and can also be dried and made into a powder for use in capsules or topical creams.
